proved: A participant has at most one active support/oppose/abstain projection per scenario while every attributed re-vote and withdrawal event remains in bounded history.
Exact event replay is idempotent and a locally reused event ID with different content is rejected before mutation.
Peer-specific versioned discussion buckets preserve both disconnected participants' first votes across forty duplicate/reordered deliveries without claiming the entire discussions collection.
Concurrent re-votes by one participant retain both alternatives and select the timestamp/event-ordered current value across another forty duplicate/reordered deliveries.
A conflicting event ID introduced by disconnected peers, malformed bucket shape, and missing scenario target fail closed or surface as explicit integrity issues.
MCP research-state inspection returns aggregate vote counts/event totals and integrity only; synthetic voter display-name content is omitted.
P-19 is implemented_unverified; no voting UI, authenticated identity, trusted distributed clock, or remote-provider claim is made.
notProved: Authenticated participant identity, account membership, authorization, or Sybil resistance.
A trusted distributed timestamp policy; current winner metadata is caller-supplied with event-ID tie-breaking.
Visible vote controls, keyboard/pointer/accessibility behavior, notifications, moderation, or recovery UI.
Two-machine Drive/WebSocket collaboration or large-vote-set performance.
Flags, notes, resolution lifecycle, labels, examples, evaluation, or policy mutation.
